--- Why I'm not shipping it ---
Input chars: 许通达. The surname 许 vs 徐 are both pinyin 'Xu' — Rule 0 collision. NO source I found displays this AI researcher's name in Chinese characters:
  - Personal site, CV, GitHub, Scholar, OpenReview, DBLP, LinkedIn URL — all pinyin only
  - Baidu/Bing CN searches for both '"许通达" 清华' and '"徐通达" 清华 张亚勤 图像压缩' returned NO hits matching this person
  - Only Chinese-char hit for either spelling is `徐通达` at FAFU (Fujian Agriculture & Forestry Univ, plant biology, Zhenbiao Yang lab) — a clearly different person (different field, different institution, only 'Tongda Xu' in plant-science publications). https://fafuucrhbmc.ucr.edu/tongda-xu / https://net.fafu.edu.cn/signaling/2a/ad/c10435a273069/page.psp
  - No `许通达` char-match for anyone in AI/compression at all — the spelling appears to have effectively zero web presence with these characters

v1 failure mode (per prompt: '杨硕' Shenzhen MSU-BIT vs UC-Berkeley) applies in reverse here: I have ONE strong-pinyin candidate but cannot prove it's 许 not 徐. Shipping x.tongda@nyu.edu under 许通达 risks: (1) the person is actually 徐通达 → DNC entry wrong, (2) the input 许通达 is a different person we haven't found → leaks through dedup either way.
